task_completion.visual_evidence: The Lobby Track application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Database restore dialog or settings accessed
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box, menu, or settings page related to restoring or importing a database is open within Lobby Track.
A file selection dialog shows 'LobbyTrackDB_backup.mdb' selected from the 'C:\LobbyTrackBackup' directory.
A success message indicating the database was restored, or the main Lobby Track window displaying the newly populated visitor records.

task_completion.description: Confirmation file created in the correct directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., Notepad) is open, and the title bar or save dialog indicates the file is named 'restore_confirmation.txt' and is being saved in 'C:\LobbyTrackBackup'.

task_completion.description: Required text and record count written to the confirmation file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor contains the exact phrase 'Database restored successfully' along with a number representing the approximate count of restored records.

integrity.description: The database restoration was performed using the Lobby Track GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Lobby Track GUI for restoring the database, rather than terminal commands like 'cp' or 'mv'.

integrity.description: The agent checked the actual records in the application to get the count
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent viewing the restored records in the Lobby Track interface to determine the count before writing it to the confirmation file.

integrity.description: The confirmation file was created using a GUI text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: A graphical text editor (like Notepad) is used to write the confirmation message, rather than terminal commands like 'echo'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The backup file is located at C:\LobbyTrackBackup\LobbyTrackDB_backup.mdb. The confirmation file must be saved at C:\LobbyTrackBackup\restore_confirmation.txt and contain the exact text 'Database restored successfully' along with the approximate number of records restored.
